title: Add --all functionality to rmi
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Add --all functionality to the rmi command to remove all containers.  This does a force to remove all of the associated containers for the images too.

comments.body:
I was going back and forth about using force under the covers for the rmi -all.  I decided to do so thinking that if someone wanted to remove all images, they'd also be thinking to remove all the containers too.  I could instead throw up a warning, list the running container(s) associated with image and then continue on.  But I thought the ---all should be omni-potent.

Also I did not address Nalin's concerns about rm and non Buildah containers.  I need to do so.  I discovered an error in rm today, it needs to also remove the containers from the list of running containers associated with the image.  Got a bit lost chasing that and will address both of those in a separate PR.
@TomSweeneyRedHat In podman you have to specify podman rmi -f -a to get force, I think we should do the same for buildah.

The tests are failing due to no status coming back from rmi.  I'll have to cook up another way to validate the removals later today or tomorrow.  I also have a weird issue with alpine  in the first rmi test.
Green buttons and ready for review.  I've worked through the bats testing issues that I encountered.

:pushpin: Commit 75c31a6 has been approved by `rhatdan`
